portfolio_facts: Equinix is a global data-center REIT/operator. Its 2025 Form 10-K describes one main business: designing, building, and operating IBX and xScale data centers. The filing says the platform had about 280 data centers worldwide.
leasing_facts: The 10-K says no single customer accounted for a significant portion of revenue. That is good for risk: the story is broad demand, not one big tenant.
customer_facts: Customer names for AI leases are not listed lease-by-lease. The xScale platform serves hyperscale companies, including large cloud providers, but individual AI customer leases should not be guessed.
capacity_power_facts: Power access is a key risk and opportunity. The 10-K says AI is power-intensive and that Equinix needs enough power capacity to support expansion.
expansion_facts: Equinix has active data-center development in many markets. The clean pitch is scale, interconnection, and lower customer-concentration risk, not a single explosive AI lease.

portfolio_facts: Digital Realty's 2025 Form 10-K says the portfolio had 310 data centers across the U.S., Europe, Latin America, Africa, Asia, Australia, and Canada, with about 57.6 million rentable square feet.
leasing_facts: The 10-K says the portfolio was about 84.7% leased. It also says 769 MW of projects were underway worldwide and about 64% pre-leased.
customer_facts: The filing supports strong hyperscale and enterprise demand, but it does not give a simple public list of every AI tenant. Do not invent customer names.
capacity_power_facts: The 10-K says land and space held for or under construction could support over 3,500 MW of additional data-center capacity, including more than 1,000 MW in Northern Virginia.
expansion_facts: Digital Realty is one of the clearest large public ways to own global data-center real estate. Growth depends on leasing new MW, funding construction, power availability, and keeping leverage controlled.

portfolio_facts: Iron Mountain's 2025 Form 10-K says it operated 31 data centers across 21 global markets, directly or through unconsolidated joint ventures.
leasing_facts: The filing says about 97% of its existing 488 MW data-center capacity was leased.
customer_facts: The filing says the data-center team focuses on hyperscale customers, but it does not name a clean list of AI tenants in the extracted annual-report data.
capacity_power_facts: The 10-K says Iron Mountain had total potential data-center capacity of about 1,340 MW in land and buildings currently owned or operated.
expansion_facts: The pitch is a stable records-management base plus a growing data-center business. It is not as pure as EQIX/DLR, but the MW pipeline is meaningful.

portfolio_facts: American Tower is mainly a communications-tower REIT. Its CoreSite data-center segment had 30 operating data-center facilities across 11 U.S. markets and about 3.7 million net rentable square feet.
leasing_facts: AMT's 2025 10-K reports Data Centers segment revenue of about $1.053 billion and operating profit of about $562 million. It does not provide a simple public CoreSite tenant lease table.
customer_facts: AMT's top company-wide customers are wireless carriers, not data-center AI tenants. CoreSite customer names should not be assumed unless CoreSite/AMT publicly names them.
capacity_power_facts: The data-center business is meaningful but small compared with the tower business. Data-center capex was about $665 million in 2025.
expansion_facts: Best treated as a tower REIT with a data-center side business, not a pure AI data-center stock.

portfolio_facts: NEXTDC is a pure Australian data-center operator. Its FY25 annual report and 1H26 presentation show a growing national and regional platform for cloud, enterprise, government, and AI workloads.
leasing_facts: The 1H26 presentation shows 416.6 MW contracted utilization and 119.8 MW billing utilization, leaving a 297 MW forward order book expected to convert into future billings.
customer_facts: NEXTDC says AI deployments are growing and reports a first hyperscale customer contracted for KL1. It does not name that customer in the extracted presentation.
capacity_power_facts: The FY25 presentation says M3 planned power capacity was upgraded by 50 MW to 200 MW, M4 by 70 MW to 150 MW, and S5 by 20 MW to 80 MW. 1H26 says S4 is now about 350 MW and M4 about 150 MW.
expansion_facts: The future depends on converting the forward order book into billings, finishing S3/M3/KL1 and later S4/M4/S5, and funding growth without too much dilution or debt risk.

portfolio_facts: Keppel DC REIT's 2025 annual report says assets under management reached S$6.3 billion and portfolio occupancy was 95.8%. It is a pure-play Asia-listed data-center REIT.
leasing_facts: The annual report says WALE was 6.7 years by lettable area and 4.9 years by rental income. Portfolio rental reversion for the year was about 45%.
customer_facts: The annual report says client names cannot be matched because many lease and colocation arrangements are confidential. Do not state named customers unless another official source names them.
capacity_power_facts: The report discusses a hyperscale strategy and additional saleable power at Keppel DC Singapore 8, but it does not give a simple public MW table for every asset.
expansion_facts: Good for income and purity, but dad should check DPU, leverage, occupancy, WALE, tenant concentration, and whether hyperscale growth improves distributions.

portfolio_facts: Digital Core REIT's 2025 annual report shows a global data-center REIT backed by Digital Realty. It added exposure in Osaka and continued to manage U.S. and European assets.
leasing_facts: The annual report says WALE was 4.6 years by annualized rent. It executed about US$26 million of new and renewal leases, equal to roughly 25% of total annualized rent. About 9% of rent expires in 2026 and another 8% in 2027.
customer_facts: The Osaka asset is described as 100% leased to investment-grade customers, mainly leading global cloud providers. The report does not name those customers.
capacity_power_facts: The Osaka data center offers 19.9 MW of critical IT load. Digital Realty's Northern Virginia campus is described as supported by a substation capable of up to 150 MW.
expansion_facts: Key things to watch: re-leasing 8217 Linton Hall, occupancy, rent reversions, sponsor pipeline, and debt costs.

data_quality: Medium - official site blocked automated extraction
portfolio_facts: NetLink NBN Trust is fiber broadband infrastructure, not a data-center landlord or operator. It should stay in the list only as digital connectivity infrastructure.
leasing_facts: No data-center leasing model applies. It is not a REIT leasing halls or MW to cloud or AI customers.
customer_facts: Not applicable for a data-center tenant story.
capacity_power_facts: Power/MW data-center capacity is not the driver. The driver is regulated fiber network usage in Singapore.
expansion_facts: Future depends on regulated returns, fiber connections, broadband demand, and Singapore telecom regulation, not AI data-center leasing.

portfolio_facts: GDS's 2025 Form 20-F says it operated 90 self-developed data centers with 664,690 sqm of net floor area in service, plus third-party leased capacity of 3,593 sqm.
leasing_facts: The filing says commitment rates for area in service were 93.0% at 31 Dec 2025 and utilization was 75.5%.
customer_facts: The filing anonymizes large customers as Customer A, Customer B, and similar labels. Do not name customers unless another official source does.
capacity_power_facts: The 20-F says 73,994 sqm was under construction and total self-developed capacity in service plus under construction was 738,684 sqm. It also says estimated developable resources were about 3.7 GW.
expansion_facts: Strong physical data-center exposure, but China regulatory risk, financing, utilization, and customer concentration must be checked.

portfolio_facts: VNET's 2025 Form 20-F describes a carrier- and cloud-neutral internet data-center provider in China.
leasing_facts: The filing says hosting and related services revenue grew to RMB9.949 billion in 2025 from RMB8.259 billion in 2024 and RMB7.413 billion in 2023.
customer_facts: The annual filing does not provide a simple named AI customer list for this dashboard. Treat customers as not fully disclosed.
capacity_power_facts: At 31 Dec 2025, VNET reported 889 MW of in-service wholesale IDC capacity and 49,863 retail IDC cabinets in service.
expansion_facts: Growth depends on China cloud/AI demand, ability to obtain high-power sites and fiber, financing, utilization, and regulatory conditions.

portfolio_facts: Mapletree Industrial Trust's 2025/2026 annual report says it had 136 properties and S$8.3 billion of AUM. The portfolio included 55 North American properties, including 13 data centers held through a joint venture, 79 Singapore properties, and two Japan properties.
leasing_facts: Average overall portfolio occupancy was 91.3%. Singapore occupancy was 92.9%, Japan occupancy was 100.0%, and North American occupancy was 87.4%. Japan WALE was 13.7 years; North American WALE was 6.3 years.
customer_facts: The report discusses tenant and leasing activity but does not give a simple named hyperscaler table for this dashboard.
capacity_power_facts: MIT is an industrial REIT with a large data-center segment, not a pure MW developer. Its AUM mix shows Data Centres at 57.3% of AUM.
expansion_facts: Important positives are data-center AUM weight and Japan stability. Risks are North American lease non-renewals and backfilling older enterprise data centers outside primary markets.

portfolio_facts: Goodman's 2025 annual report says total portfolio was A$85.6 billion, occupancy was 96.5%, and WALE was 5.0 years. Development work in progress was A$12.9 billion, with 57% in data centers.
leasing_facts: Goodman does not provide a simple named tenant lease table for its data-center pipeline. The report says customers include hyperscalers and colocators needing power and fiber.
customer_facts: Customer categories are public; individual data-center customers are not listed in a clean public table in the checked sources.
capacity_power_facts: The annual report says Goodman had a 5.0 GW global data-center power bank across 13 major cities, with 2.7 GW secured and 2.3 GW in advanced procurement. It also had 130 MW of fully fitted data-center projects underway.
expansion_facts: This is a high-quality industrial property developer/manager with huge data-center power optionality, but not a pure data-center REIT.

portfolio_facts: Azrieli is diversified real estate, but its data-center segment is real and mainly through Green Mountain. The 2025 annual report says the group operates in malls, offices, senior housing, rental housing, overseas real estate, and data centers.
leasing_facts: Azrieli's Q4 2025 presentation says Green Mountain Global had 257 MW contracted, 148 MW billed, and 109 MW billed-but-not-booked across 7 sites in 3 countries.
customer_facts: The 2025 annual report names TikTok Norway AS as the customer for a Green Mountain Norway DC services agreement.
capacity_power_facts: The annual report says Green Mountain built a 90 MW campus in Norway for TikTok Norway AS and completed handover of the full 90 MW in 2024. It also says the customer exercised an option to increase capacity by another 30 MW to 120 MW, but that expansion was still under review.
expansion_facts: This is one of the more specific indirect data-center stories: named customer, MW, and contracted MW. But the public ticker is still a diversified real-estate group.

portfolio_facts: Keppel Ltd is a diversified asset manager/operator, not a pure data-center REIT. Its 2025 annual report says the Connectivity division expanded the data-center portfolio to over 800 MW in gross power capacity.
leasing_facts: Keppel's report does not give a public ticker-level tenant lease table. Data-center leases sit in projects, funds, and REIT/fund vehicles.
customer_facts: No simple named customer table is provided for Keppel Ltd shareholders in the annual report. Do not assume tenant names.
capacity_power_facts: The report says Keppel expanded its Asia-Pacific data-center powerbank to over 1.0 GW, including a prime site near Melbourne earmarked for a future 720 MW AI data-center campus.
expansion_facts: The pitch is powerbanking plus asset-management fees and capital recycling. It is indirect data-center exposure, with broader infrastructure risks.

portfolio_facts: Macquarie Technology Group owns and operates Australian data-center, cloud, government, cyber, and telecom infrastructure. The 2025 annual report breaks out Macquarie Data Centres as a key business.
leasing_facts: The FY25 presentation says 2 out of 3 major hyperscalers are customers. It also says IC3 Super West is on time and on budget.
customer_facts: The presentation names customer groups, including hyperscalers, enterprise, government, SaaS companies, and carriers. It does not name the hyperscalers in the extracted text.
capacity_power_facts: The annual report says IC3 Super West Phase 1 will deliver 6 MW of IT load by September 2026 and the facility has 47 MW approved capacity. It also says a new Sydney campus is expected to deliver more than 150 MW of IT load.
expansion_facts: This is a specific Australia data-center buildout story. Watch IC3 Super West delivery, 150+ MW campus approvals, funding, and customer conversion.

portfolio_facts: Applied Digital's FY2025 Form 10-K says it designs, develops, and operates data-center infrastructure for HPC and AI in North America. It has blockchain hosting and HPC hosting segments; cloud services were held for sale.
leasing_facts: The 10-K says its Data Center Hosting segment served one crypto mining customer with about 2.5 years remaining and generated all FY2025 continuing revenue. For HPC, it entered two lease agreements for the first two Polaris Forge 1 buildings totaling 250 MW.
customer_facts: The filing supports signed HPC leasing, but it does not name the two HPC lease customers in the extracted text.
capacity_power_facts: It operated Jamestown, ND at 106 MW and Ellendale, ND at 180 MW, about 286 MW total hosting capacity. Polaris Forge 1 is planned as 100 MW, 150 MW, and 150 MW buildings.
expansion_facts: Big upside if Polaris Forge 1 converts to cash flow; high risk because current revenue was still crypto-hosting heavy in FY2025 and capex is large.

data_quality: High - SEC 10-K plus official company release
portfolio_facts: IREN's FY2025 Form 10-K says it has three Texas sites with grid connection agreements: Childress, Sweetwater 1, and Sweetwater 2. Childress is a 750 MW site with about 650 MW operating data-center capacity as of 30 Jun 2025.
leasing_facts: The annual report says IREN is expanding into AI Cloud and possible colocation services. A later IREN company release on 3 Nov 2025 says Microsoft signed a five-year GPU cloud services contract with about $9.7 billion total contract value, including a 20% prepayment.
customer_facts: The FY2025 annual report does not name Microsoft. The later IREN company release names Microsoft. Treat Microsoft as confirmed by the company release, not by the annual report.
capacity_power_facts: The 10-K says Horizon 1 at Childress is a direct-to-chip liquid-cooling deployment with up to 50 MW IT load, targeting energization by the end of calendar 2025. The IREN release says Microsoft GPUs are expected to deploy through 2026 at Childress with new liquid-cooled data centers supporting 200 MW of critical IT load across Horizon 1-4.
expansion_facts: Very high upside because power-secured sites can move from bitcoin mining to AI/HPC. Very high risk because it needs huge GPU/capex funding and the stock may already price in a lot.

portfolio_facts: TeraWulf's 2025 Form 10-K says it is building a contracted HPC platform from large power/data-center campuses, not only bitcoin mining.
leasing_facts: The filing says Lake Mariner and Abernathy have contracted 522 MW of critical IT load with long-dated revenue visibility.
customer_facts: The filing references credit-enhanced counterparties but the extracted text should not be used to claim a specific named end customer. Do not rely on XBRL labels alone for customer names.
capacity_power_facts: The filing says the Cayuga site could support up to 400 MW gross capacity, or about 320 MW contractual critical IT load, after permitting and development.
expansion_facts: This is a speculative AI/HPC power-secured name. It can score well on physical power pipeline but must be flagged high risk because execution, financing, and customer concentration matter.

portfolio_facts: Core Scientific's 2025 Form 10-K says it owned or leased 10 data centers in seven U.S. states with about 1.4 GW gross utility power capacity and 920 MW total leasable customer power capacity.
leasing_facts: The filing says CoreWeave leases grew from a 16 MW Austin contract to 200 MW and then to about 590 MW of leased power capacity through options.
customer_facts: CoreWeave is a named public customer in the 10-K.
capacity_power_facts: The company says it intends to convert every MW in its portfolio to high-density colocation infrastructure over the next three years while continuing mining during conversion.
expansion_facts: This is one of the clearest crypto-to-HPC conversion stories, but it is still risky because 2025 revenue was mostly digital assets and the company has bankruptcy history.

portfolio_facts: Hut 8's 2025 Form 10-K reorganized the business into Power, Digital Infrastructure, Compute, and Other.
leasing_facts: The filing does not show a simple AI lease table yet. It is more of a power and site-development story.
customer_facts: No named AI data-center tenant is confirmed in the extracted 10-K text for this dashboard row.
capacity_power_facts: The 10-K says Hut 8 had 1,020 MW of energy capacity under management across 15 sites. It is developing an AI data center at River Bend in Louisiana to commercialize 330 MW of utility capacity, targeted for initial delivery and commissioning in Q2 2027.
expansion_facts: Hut 8 also says it is advancing commercialization of 1,230 MW of utility capacity under development. Good power optionality, but very speculative until AI leases are clearer.

data_quality: Medium-high - SEC 20-F; named AI lease not confirmed in filing
portfolio_facts: Bitdeer's 2025 Form 20-F is still heavily tied to bitcoin mining, mining rigs, datacenters, and a growing AI infrastructure / AI cloud effort. It acquired Troll Housing AS and Tydal Data Center AS in Norway in 2024.
leasing_facts: The filing warns that delays in converting mining datacenters to AI datacenters or building new datacenters could hurt the business. A large named AI customer lease was not confirmed in the extracted 20-F.
customer_facts: The annual filing does not give a clean named AI tenant list in the extracted text. Do not claim a named customer without a company release.
capacity_power_facts: The filing confirms the Norway/Tydal data-center acquisition and defines colocation, AI, GPU, HPC, and MW. The filing does not give a clean current AI MW lease table by site.
expansion_facts: This is a high-risk bitcoin-miner-to-AI-infrastructure transition. It needs capital, power, build execution, and real AI leases.

portfolio_facts: DigitalBridge's 2025 Form 10-K says it is a leading global investment manager in digital infrastructure, deploying capital across data centers, cell towers, and fiber networks.
leasing_facts: Leasing happens at portfolio-company level, not directly at the public ticker level.
customer_facts: No simple DBRG-level data-center customer list applies.
capacity_power_facts: The filing says DBRG had $41.0 billion of fee-earning equity under management at 31 Dec 2025. It is indirect exposure, not a data-center REIT with public MW leasing.
expansion_facts: Use DBRG as an asset-manager/data-infrastructure exposure, not as a direct landlord comparison to EQIX/DLR.

portfolio_facts: Blackstone's 2025 Form 10-K says its real-estate funds have made significant investments in logistics, data centers, rental housing, hospitality, office, retail, and real-estate operating companies.
leasing_facts: Data-center leases are held through funds and portfolio companies, not directly at BX shareholder level.
customer_facts: No ticker-level data-center tenant list applies.
capacity_power_facts: The filing discusses digital infrastructure risks, including data-center competition, AI technology changes, and local opposition.
expansion_facts: BX is very indirect. It can benefit from the data-center boom, but the stock is driven by the whole alternative-asset-management business.

portfolio_facts: Brookfield Asset Management's 2025 Form 10-K defines Data4 and says its infrastructure investments include businesses that transmit and store data globally, including telecom towers, fiber optic cable, and data centers.
leasing_facts: Data-center leasing is inside funds/portfolio companies, not at BAM public-company level.
customer_facts: No BAM-level data-center tenant list applies.
capacity_power_facts: The annual report confirms exposure to digital infrastructure and data centers, but not a public MW table for BAM shareholders.
expansion_facts: BAM is an asset-light manager. It is a broad manager exposure to infrastructure, not a pure data-center stock.

portfolio_facts: Brookfield Corporation is the broader Brookfield parent/holding company. Its filing shows broad infrastructure, real estate, renewable power, private equity, and asset-management exposure.
leasing_facts: Data-center leases are not direct BN-level disclosures. They sit in operating companies, funds, and investees.
customer_facts: No BN-level data-center tenant list applies.
capacity_power_facts: The filing includes infrastructure and network infrastructure categories, but not a clean public data-center MW table for BN.
expansion_facts: BN is broad and diluted. It may benefit from Brookfield's data-center and power themes, but it is not a focused data-center pitch.

portfolio_facts: KKR is a broad alternative asset manager with real assets, infrastructure, credit, insurance, and private equity exposure. Its 2025 10-K is not a focused data-center disclosure.
leasing_facts: Any data-center leasing sits inside funds/portfolio companies, not at KKR public ticker level.
customer_facts: No KKR-level data-center tenant list applies.
capacity_power_facts: The filing supports infrastructure/real-assets exposure, but it does not provide a clean data-center MW or lease table.
expansion_facts: KKR is useful only as indirect exposure. It should not be compared directly with pure operators like EQIX, DLR, NEXTDC, or Digital Core.
